D5 Design L4 Analyze Reference

Dark Mode Trade-offs

light-on-dark saves light at night and OLED power — but blurry-eyed readers see the text halate and bloom

Dark mode emits less light (easier at night, and cheaper on OLED where black pixels are off). But for the very common slightly-astigmatic or myopic eye, bright text on a dark field blooms: the wide-open pupil and imperfect focus smear each stroke into a halo, so paragraphs can actually read worse than dark-on-light.

Toggle the mode and raise the focus blur to stand in for an uncorrected eye. Dark-on-light stays legible as it blurs; light-on-dark haloes and the counters fill in. Dark mode is a preference, not a universal improvement.
